        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)}), children={n['children']}")

for i, h in enumerate(history):

    if small_charcoal:
        positions = [(n['boundary'][0][0], n['boundary'][0][1]) for n in small_charcoal]

The output was truncated, but I can see the pattern. The charcoal squares appear and disappear as I move around. The one at (28,16) keeps disappearing and reappearing. The one at (63,*) is the HUD bar, not a game object.
Let me focus on the key insight: the charcoal squares at (24,44) and (28,16) are the game objects. The one at (24,20) appears sometimes - that's probably a different position of the same object.
Looking at the current state, I have:
- Merged object at (24,44)-(31,47) with 2 blue centers (24 pixels of charcoal)
- Left square at (28,28)-(31,39) with gray center
- Player at (52,32)-(54,35)
- White strip at (55,32)-(59,35) with blue center
The initial state had 3 charcoal squares. Currently, there's only 1 merged object with 2 blue centers. The third charcoal square is missing.
Wait, looking at the history more carefully, the charcoal squares at (24,20) and (28,16) seem to be the same object moving around. The one at (24,44) is stationary. So there are 2 charcoal squares: one at (24,44) and one that moves around.
But the current merged object has 2 blue centers and 24 pixels of charcoal, which is 2x the size of a single charcoal square (12 pixels). So 2 charcoal squares have been merged.
But where's the third one? Looking at the initial state again:
- id=1: c at (24,44)-(27,47) - stationary
- id=3: c at (28,16)-(31,19) - movable
- id=7: c at (36,32)-(39,35) - movable
So there are 3 charcoal squares initially. The one at (24,44) is stationary, and the other 2 are movable.
Looking at the history, after step 1, the charcoal at (36,32) disappears. This is probably because the player pushed it into the one at (24,44), merging them.
So now I have:
- Merged object at (24,44)-(31,47) with 2 blue centers (2 merged charcoal squares)
- One remaining charcoal square at (28,16) or (24,20) - this is the third one
- Blue square at (28,28)-(31,39) with gray center
But in the current state, I don't see the third charcoal square! Let me check more carefully.
Actually, looking at the current state output:
- id=1: c at (24,44)-(31,47) - merged object with 2 blue centers
- id=3: b at (28,28)-(31,39) - blue square with gray center
- id=6: N at (52,32)-(54,35) - player
- id=7: W at (55,32)-(59,35) - white strip with blue center
There's no third charcoal square. It must have been destroyed or transformed at some point.
Wait, maybe the white strip with blue center IS the third square, transformed. The initial charcoal squares had blue centers, and the white strip also has a blue center. Maybe when the player picks up a charcoal square with SPACE, it transforms into a white strip with a blue center.
So the goal might be to:
1. Push the white strip (which is the third charcoal square) into the merged object
2. This would create a 3-blue-center merged object
3. Then push the blue square with gray center into the merged object
4. This would complete the level
Let me try pushing the white strip into the merged object. The merged object is at (24,44)-(31,47), and the white strip is at (55,32)-(59,35). I need to move the player so the white strip is adjacent to the merged object, then push it in.
The white strip is below the player (rows 55-59 vs 52-54). To push the white strip UP into the merged object, I need to move DOWN (which would push the white strip up... no, that doesn't make sense).
Actually, the white strip is attached to the player. When the player moves, the white strip moves with the player. The white strip is in the opposite direction of the last movement.
Last movement was LEFT, so the white strip is to the RIGHT of the player. But looking at the positions, the player is at (52,32)-(54,35) and the white strip is at (55,32)-(59,35). The white strip is below the player, not to the right.
Hmm, maybe the white strip's position depends on more than just the last movement direction. Let me just try moving UP to get the white strip closer to the merged object.

- id=1: c at (24,44)-(31,47) - merged object with 2 blue centers
- id=3: b at (28,28)-(31,39) - blue square with gray center
- id=6: N at (52,32)-(54,35) - player
- id=7: W at (55,32)-(59,35) - white strip with blue center
There's no third charcoal square. It must have been destroyed or transformed at some point.
Wait, maybe the white strip with blue center IS the third square, transformed. The initial charcoal squares had blue centers, and the white strip also has a blue center. Maybe when the player picks up a charcoal square with SPACE, it transforms into a white strip with a blue center.
So the goal might be to:
1. Push the white strip (which is the third charcoal square) into the merged object
2. This would create a 3-blue-center merged object
3. Then push the blue square with gray center into the merged object
4. This would complete the level
Let me try pushing the white strip into the merged object. The merged object is at (24,44)-(31,47), and the white strip is at (55,32)-(59,35). I need to move the player so the white strip is adjacent to the merged object, then push it in.
The white strip is below the player (rows 55-59 vs 52-54). To push the white strip UP into the merged object, I need to move DOWN (which would push the white strip up... no, that doesn't make sense).
Actually, the white strip is attached to the player. When the player moves, the white strip moves with the player. The white strip is in the opposite direction of the last movement.
Last movement was LEFT, so the white strip is to the RIGHT of the player. But looking at the positions, the player is at (52,32)-(54,35) and the white strip is at (55,32)-(59,35). The white strip is below the player, not to the right.
Hmm, maybe the white strip's position depends on more than just the last movement direction. Let me just try moving UP to get the white strip closer to the merged object.
